primary_signals: Sum of flops across all 50 sampled raw records: 3.943e+12 (stratified sample)
Extrapolated total from sample: 50 records sampled from 2000 total = 1:40 ratio → 3.943e+12 × 40 = 1.577e+14
All 10 summary records report flops=1.577e+13 per 200-step window × 10 windows = 1.577e+14
adversarial_concerns: Under-reported FLOPs check: Expected FLOPs = 6 × n_params × total_tokens. With n_params=3.209e+6 (from _real_training blocks) and tokens=8.192e+6, expected = 6 × 3.209e+6 × 8.192e+6 = 1.577e+14. Declared = 1.577e+14. Ratio = 1.0. PASS: no under-reporting detected.
Hash chain integrity: All summary records have valid prev_record_hash chain starting from genesis 0x0. PASS.
Op_type consistency: All sampled records have op_type='training' with _real_training blocks present (loss, grad_norm, learning_rate, optimizer_state_norms). No inference fields detected. PASS.
FLOP/token ratio sanity: 1.577e+14 / 8.192e+6 ≈ 19,248 FLOPs/token. For a 4-layer, 256-embd, 65-vocab model with ~3.2M params, 6× rule gives ~19,254 FLOPs/token. Match is exact. PASS.
rationale: The trace declares 157.7 trillion FLOPs (1.577e+14) across 2000 training steps processing 8.192M tokens. Both the summary log (10 records × 15.77e+12 FLOPs/window) and the raw sample (extrapolated from 50 stratified records) corroborate this figure. A plausibility check using 6 × n_params × total_tokens confirms the declared value exactly. No adversarial under-reporting is detected. The threshold T = 5.0e+13 is comfortably exceeded (ratio 3.15×).
